source.title: How Many Packages Are De Minimis Shipments? Stats for 2026

extractions.claim: US daily de minimis volume crashed ~85% from ~4M to ~600K packages/day after policy elimination
extractions.evidence: Daily de minimis volume saw a decline 'upwards of 85%,' according to Chris Mabelitini, director of U.S. CBP's intellectual property rights and e-commerce division. Volume went from almost 4 million packages per day to around 600,000 per day.

verification.verifier_notes: CBP official statement; represents aggregate China-US e-commerce decline

extractions.claim: De minimis elimination increased per-package logistics costs by an estimated 30-50%
extractions.evidence: The elimination of the exemption increased per-package logistics costs by an estimated 30% to 50%. Industry estimates suggest that SHEIN and Temu alone were shipping over one million packages per day to the US via air before the policy change.

verification.verifier_notes: Industry estimate from Supply Chain Dive and McKinsey analysis

extractions.claim: Temu's US GMV recovered to 60%+ of pre-tariff levels by July 2025 after initial sharp decline
extractions.evidence: By July 2025, Temu's GMV recovered to more than 60% of past levels, following a pattern of sharp drop followed by quick recovery consistent with earlier trade disruptions.

verification.verifier_notes: Analyst estimate; not official PDD disclosure

extractions.claim: China-to-US e-commerce air exports fell 28% for full year 2025, with 50%+ declines in final months
extractions.evidence: For full year 2025, China-to-US e-commerce air exports were down 28% compared to the prior year. E-commerce exports extended their steep decline, down more than 50% for a third consecutive month in December.

verification.verifier_notes: From S&P Global and aviation industry analysis

extractions.claim: EU plans to eliminate EUR 150 de minimis threshold, moved forward from 2028 to 2026 with interim EUR 3/item fee starting July 1, 2026
extractions.evidence: In the EU, plans to remove the EUR 150 de minimis threshold are being brought forward from 2028 to 2026. With an interim EUR 3 fee per item starting July 1, 2026, the 'Temu model' of shipping $5 trinkets becomes economically impossible.

verification.verifier_notes: EU regulatory announcements; EUR 3 fee is proposed, may change
